ATL.operator: City of Atlanta Department of Aviation
ATL.note: Busiest airport in the US by enplanements in 2000; Delta's largest hub.

ORD.operator: City of Chicago Department of Aviation
ORD.note: United and American Airlines hub; among the world's busiest airports.

LAX.operator: Los Angeles World Airports (City of Los Angeles)
LAX.note: Primary gateway for trans-Pacific and international West Coast traffic.

DFW.note: Joint city of Dallas/Fort Worth airport; American Airlines' largest hub.

SFO.operator: City and County of San Francisco
SFO.note: United Airlines hub and major trans-Pacific gateway.

DEN.note: Replaced the old Stapleton International Airport in 1995; United hub.

LAS.note: Primary air gateway for Las Vegas tourism.

EWR.operator: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey

MIA.note: American Airlines hub and primary US gateway to Latin America and the Caribbean.

IAH.operator: City of Houston Department of Aviation

JFK.operator: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey
JFK.note: Primary New York gateway for international/transatlantic flights.

STL.note: Trans World Airlines (TWA) hub through the 2001 American Airlines acquisition.

MCO.note: Primary gateway for Central Florida tourism/theme parks.

BOS.note: American Airlines Flight 11 and United Airlines Flight 175 departed from Boston Logan on 2001-09-11.

LGA.operator: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey
LGA.note: Slot-controlled airport serving primarily domestic short-haul routes.

PHL.operator: City of Philadelphia Division of Aviation

CVG.note: Delta Air Lines hub, though located in Boone County, Kentucky rather than Cincinnati proper.

HNL.note: Primary gateway for the Hawaiian Islands.

PIT.note: US Airways hub in 2000; enplanements later fell sharply after US Airways de-hubbed Pittsburgh.

IAD.note: United Airlines hub; terminal designed by Eero Saarinen.

SLC.operator: Salt Lake City Department of Airports

DCA.note: Slot- and perimeter-restricted; closed for several days after 2001-09-11 and reopened under tightened security.

MDW.operator: City of Chicago Department of Aviation
MDW.note: Southwest Airlines' Chicago base; enplanements grew rapidly through the late 1990s.

CLE.operator: City of Cleveland Department of Port Control

MCI.operator: City of Kansas City Aviation Department

MEM.note: Northwest Airlines passenger hub and the FedEx global air-cargo superhub.

MSY.operator: City of New Orleans Aviation Board

HOU.operator: City of Houston Department of Aviation
HOU.note: Southwest Airlines' primary Houston base; downtown-adjacent alternative to IAH.

SNA.note: Known as John Wayne Airport; noise-restricted.

AUS.note: Opened 1999 at the former Bergstrom Air Force Base, replacing Robert Mueller Municipal Airport.

DAL.note: Southwest Airlines' original headquarters base; Wright Amendment flight restrictions applied in 2000.

SAT.operator: City of San Antonio Aviation Department

ONT.operator: City of Los Angeles (Los Angeles World Airports)
ONT.note: Operated as part of the LAX system in 2000.

PBI.operator: Palm Beach County Department of Airports

ANC.operator: State of Alaska Department of Transportation
ANC.note: Major air-cargo transfer hub between Asia and North America.
